text:
The parties and their legal advisers (if any) shall [attend the court building] / [communicate with each other] at least one hour prior to the listing time of the hearing to negotiate and attempt to narrow the issues.

Evidence at the next hearing

The parties shall produce an electronic hearing bundle which complies with:

FPR 2010 PD 27A; and

paragraphs 81 and 82 of the Financial Remedies Guide of 13 March 2026.

The [applicant] / [respondent] shall prepare a bundle containing:

an updated composite case summary using Template ES1;

an updated composite schedule of assets using Template ES2;

a composite chronology recording in neutral terms the key dates of the parties’ relationship and of the litigation and where any unagreed events are clearly denoted;

an [agreed] summary of the issues to be determined;

[insert (list other documents to be included – e.g. ‘all applications and orders made in these proceedings’, ‘the parties’ narrative Forms E’, ‘the parties’ narrative replies to questionnaire’, ‘the parties’ etc. narrative witness statements’, ‘the expert reports’)]

The bundle shall be agreed [if possible] by both parties. The [applicant] / [respondent] shall send the [respondent] / [applicant] a draft index for the bundle by [time and date] / [not later than [number] days before the [financial dispute resolution appointment] / [final hearing]], and the [respondent] / [applicant] shall send the [applicant] / [respondent] any comments on the index by [time and date] / [not later than [number] days before the [financial dispute resolution appointment] / [final hearing]].

The bundle must be paginated. [It shall contain no more than 350 pages]. / [Permission is given for the bundle to exceed 350 pages, but shall contain no more than [number] pages.]

The [applicant] / [respondent] shall file the bundle with the court by [time and date] / [not later than [number] days before the [financial dispute resolution appointment] / [final hearing]], and shall provide a copy of the bundle to the [respondent] / [applicant] by [time and date] / [not later than [number] days before the [financial dispute resolution appointment] / [final hearing]].

metadata.source: Standard Family Orders — Volume 1: Financial Remedies (May 2026)
